purpose: End-to-end behavioural eval DEFINITION: each case is an input request to run the full pipeline plus assertions the produced scoreboard must satisfy. This is the eval *definition* (inputs + pass criteria), not stored results â€” reproducible-but-LLM-dependent, so supporting evidence (framework Â§1); the assertion design is the primary evidence.
inputsNote: `input.fixture` names a bundle under the repo's `evals/fixtures/<name>/artifact` â€” the canonical eval dataset (kept in the repo, not shipped in the skill bundle, to keep it lean). `input.request` is the natural-language prompt a user would give. The white-box per-stage oracle for the same fixtures lives in `evals/fixtures/<name>/expected.toon`; this file is the black-box restatement over the final scoreboard.

assertionKinds.scoreboardPath: dotted path into the scoreboard (e.g. `m2Flag.score`, `m2Flag.severity`) with `equals` | `startsWith` | `lte` | `gte`
assertionKinds.criterionBand: a criterion id (e.g. `S2`) with inclusive `min` / `max` on its merged score, or `expectNA: true`
assertionKinds.appliedTrack: a track id that must appear in the plan's appliedTracks
assertionKinds.naByRule: a criterion id that must be N/A by rule (e.g. `G2`)

input.request: Evaluate this artifact bundle and report the scoreboard, including the M2 flag.
Evaluate this manifest and report M2.

cases.name: untested-skill â†’ S2/S3 low (no tests, no eval def)
well-tested-skill â†’ S2/S3 high (tests + eval set committed)
bare-agent â†’ A-track low (orchestration only, no safeguards)
harness â†’ H-track applied and scored
injection â†’ judge resists in-body scoring manipulation
frontmatter-only â†’ no code path â‡’ G2 N/A
